notes.anchor: the day before the Ides of June
notes.note: Romans counted dates inclusively backwards from three fixed points each month; the Ides of June fell on the 13th, so the day before is 12 June. Written from Brundisium in 47 BC, where Cicero was stranded after Pharsalus awaiting Caesar's pardon.

notes.anchor: send our Cicero to Caesar, and Gnaeus Sallustius with him
notes.note: Cicero planned to send his teenage son Marcus to intercede with the victorious Caesar, escorted by his friend Gnaeus Sallustius; the embassy was meant to secure the family's safety after the Pompeian defeat.
